content: A large language model predicts tokens from context and can be adapted for chat, summarization, coding, and classification tasks.
Transformers use self-attention to model relationships between tokens. Attention enables long-range dependencies and efficient parallel training.
Embeddings map text into vectors where semantic similarity is represented by geometric proximity. Retrieval systems compare vectors to find relevant context.
Approximate nearest neighbor methods trade exactness for speed and scale. They are commonly used in vector databases that support low-latency semantic lookup.
In retrieval-augmented generation, retrieval selects candidate passages and the generator synthesizes an answer grounded in those passages.
